name: Blender Open MCP
what_it_does: Blender Open MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Blender — the professional open-source 3D creation suite — creating and modifying 3D objects, controlling materials and lighting, managing scene hierarchies, and integrating AI-driven 3D modeling and scene creation into agent workflows for animation, visualization, and game asset creation.
use_cases: Generating 3D scenes from natural language descriptions from creative agents
Modifying 3D object properties and transforms from design agents
Creating materials and shaders for 3D objects from artistic agents
Animating objects and cameras from storytelling agents
Generating game-ready assets from game development agents
not_for: Teams not using Blender (use Maya, Cinema4D, or other 3D software MCPs)
Simple 2D image generation (use DALL-E or Stable Diffusion MCPs)
best_when: A 3D artist or developer uses Blender and wants AI assistance generating and modifying 3D scenes — using natural language to control Blender's powerful 3D creation capabilities.
avoid_when: You don't use Blender or need real-time 3D without a running Blender instance.

auth.notes: No authentication — communicates with Blender via Python scripting interface. Blender must be running with the MCP addon enabled.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Blender and MCP addon are completely free and open source
pricing.notes: Blender is free open source 3D software. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: Local Blender — limited by 3D scene complexity

known_agent_gotchas: Blender must be running with MCP addon installed and activated
Complex operations can be slow — rendering especially takes significant time
LLM needs Blender Python API knowledge to generate effective commands
Community MCP — Blender Python API is extensive but complex
Blender version compatibility must be verified for addon
3D scene state is mutable — operations may be hard to undo
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local Blender Python. No credentials. Community MCP. Python execution within Blender process.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local Python execution in Blender. No credentials. Community MCP.
